name: Wayve
growth: Series D round of $1.2B+ closed Feb 2026; valuation 3x in under two years

tearSheet.thesis: The flagship expression of firstminute's top 2026 conviction: embodied and physical AI. Wayve has crossed from research credibility to commercial deployment, with OEM partnerships confirmed and a Series D that closed at roughly $1.5B in early 2026.
tearSheet.edge: End-to-end learning that generalises across new cities without HD maps, a fundamental architectural advantage over rule-based competitors. The Uber and Stellantis global L4 robotaxi alliance plus a Nissan production deal confirm the commercial thesis.
tearSheet.signal: On thesis. Directly named as the flagship embodied AI bet in the firstminute portfolio. Nvidia, Microsoft, SoftBank, Uber, and three automakers on the cap table is board-level validation.
tearSheet.risk: Capital intensity: burning roughly $77M per year. The live ownership question is dilution in mega-rounds, not relevance. The road to scaled commercial revenue is long, and Waymo outspends by a wide margin.
tearSheet.action: Defend ownership. Exercise full pro-rata and pursue super pro-rata where the round structure allows. The seed stake at 5.80% is the position to protect.

founders.name: Alex Kendall Ph.D
founders.title: Co-Founder and CEO
founders.background: Ph.D. Cambridge, Computer Vision and Robotics. Pioneer of end-to-end deep learning for autonomous vehicles. Founded Wayve in 2017.

founders.title: Co-Founder
founders.background: Co-founder, also an angel in CodeWords (another portfolio company).

funding.leads: Financial investors; Nvidia, Microsoft, Uber, Stellantis, Mercedes-Benz, Nissan
Stemar Capital, Reference Capital, firstminute Capital

firstminute.role: One of three seed investors in the original $2.08M round

financials.notes: Burning roughly $77M/yr but holds a fortress balance sheet of about $1.19B in assets post-Series D, with effectively zero debt. Capital intensity is real; the balance sheet absorbs it.

competitors.name: Waymo
competitors.notes: Dominant US player, far larger budget

signals.detail: Press-driven traffic from global OEM partnership announcements. Seven global offices, 11 patent families.

Self-driving startup Wayve raises $1.2B from Nvidia, Uber, and three automakers
Stellantis, Wayve and Uber partner to scale global L4 robotaxis
Wayve and Uber to launch L4 autonomy trials in London

name: Mistral AI
growth: Annualised revenue run-rate passed $400M in 2025; in talks to raise EUR 3B at EUR 20B

tearSheet.thesis: Europe's foundation-model champion and the single strongest sovereignty asset in the portfolio. Backed at seed in Jun 2023 for $113M, one of the largest European seed rounds ever. The company is now in talks to roughly double its valuation to EUR 20B.
tearSheet.edge: Three ex-DeepMind and ex-Meta AI co-founders who collectively hold the majority of shares, maintaining near-total control. The EU AI Act and data-residency rules create structural, non-discretionary demand for a credible European model provider.
tearSheet.signal: On thesis. firstminute's published commentary names Mistral as a core European AI holding. The $830M debt raise for GPU procurement and persistent Apple acquisition talks signal the company is at an inflection.
tearSheet.risk: Brutal compute and capital intensity competing with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. The debate is margin economics, not relevance. A 54% M&A probability means an exit may come before an IPO.
tearSheet.action: Treat as a strategic anchor position. Protect pro-rata on principle, beyond the return math alone. The Apple acquisition signal warrants a board-level conversation about preferred exit path.

alerts.message: In talks to raise EUR 3B at a EUR 20B valuation (Jun 2026), nearly double the Sep 2025 Series C price. Apple acquisition interest reported on and off since 2025.

founders.name: Arthur Mensch
founders.title: Co-Founder and CEO
founders.background: Ex-Google DeepMind. Architect of the Mistral 7B open-weight model. Leads commercial strategy.

founders.name: Timothee Lacroix
founders.title: Co-Founder and CTO
founders.background: Ex-Meta AI (FAIR). Deep expertise in large-scale distributed training systems.

founders.name: Guillaume Lample
founders.title: Co-Founder and Chief Scientist
founders.background: Ex-Meta AI (FAIR), lead author of LLaMA. One of the most cited ML researchers in Europe.

funding.leads: ASML (lead, ~11% stake), Nvidia, DST Global, a16z

firstminute.role: Seed investor, one of many in the $113M seed

financials.notes: Run-rate passed $400M in 2025. The $830M debt was raised specifically to fund Mistral Compute, targeting up to 200MW of capacity by 2027 and 1GW by 2030. The three co-founders retain majority control.

signals.detail: Roughly 598K unique weekly visitors and 19.2K referring domains. Codestral and Le Chat are driving enterprise adoption.

news.headline: Mistral AI in funding talks at a EUR 20B valuation
Mistral AI raises EUR 1.7B Series C; ASML takes ~11% stake for EUR 1.3B
Mistral nearly doubles valuation to EUR 20B as Europe's AI race heats up

name: n8n
growth: 6x user growth and 10x revenue growth in 2025; became a unicorn in six years

tearSheet.thesis: firstminute's most recent unicorn, reached after the original $1.49M seed co-led with Sequoia. Open-source, agent-native workflow automation now at $5.2B post-money after SAP's strategic investment in May 2026.
tearSheet.edge: About 1.9M weekly unique visitors driven by a developer community. The open-source model compounds: every integration the community builds strengthens the platform moat. SAP's investment confirms the strategic-acquirer profile.
tearSheet.signal: Strongest beneficiary of AI agent orchestration becoming critical enterprise infrastructure. 6x user and 10x revenue growth in 2025, plus Mercedes-Benz and SAP partnerships, confirm enterprise-tier adoption.
tearSheet.risk: Sam Endacott, the lead partner who championed n8n at firstminute, left the firm in June 2026 to join Northzone. That is a material relationship-continuity risk. M&A probability sits at 71%, with SAP the likely buyer.
tearSheet.action: Exercise full pro-rata into growth rounds. A high-confidence compounding position. Resolve the internal relationship-continuity question before the next board interaction.

alerts.message: Per the company capture, Sam Endacott (the firstminute partner who led n8n) is reported to have moved to Northzone in 2026. Verify internally; if accurate, the lead-partner relationship has changed.

founders.name: Jan Oberhauser
founders.title: Founder, CEO and Managing Director
founders.background: Single founder. His holding entity Oberhauser Ventures owns 46.20%, plus 1.95% personally, for total founder control of about 48%.

firstminute.ownershipNote: Firstminute Capital I LP as of Dec 2024

financials.notes: Extremely capital-efficient open-source model. Only about $7M in total assets despite a $5.2B valuation, reflecting near-zero infrastructure spend. The Series B-to-C step-up was roughly 8.6x in seven months.

signals.detail: Stable in the 1.8M to 2.3M weekly range. Mercedes-Benz partnership on an AI workflow platform; Deutsche Telekom fast-track to AI agents.

news.headline: SAP bets big on AI, invests in n8n at a $5.2B valuation
Nvidia backs $180M round for n8n at $2.5B valuation
Fair-code pioneer n8n raises $60M for AI-powered workflow automation

name: Granola
growth: Seed to $1.5B unicorn in under three years; Series B to C was 6x in ten months

tearSheet.thesis: Held since the $4.25M seed in April 2023. Granola reached unicorn status at $1.5B in March 2026, seed to unicorn in under three years. It is at the inflection from a beloved individual tool to an enterprise platform, now backed by Index Ventures and Kleiner Perkins.
tearSheet.edge: The strongest angel syndicate in the portfolio: Tobi Lutke, Guillermo Rauch, Amjad Masad, Mike Krieger. These are active product advocates inside their own organisations. Enterprise logos now include Vanta, Gusto, Asana, and Mistral.
tearSheet.signal: Opportunity Score 93/100, the highest in the entire portfolio, above Wayve and Tessl. Competitors are explicitly copying the approach: Fathom added a bot-less mode in a direct bid to replicate Granola's UX.
tearSheet.risk: firstminute did not participate in Series B ($43M) or Series C ($125M). The window to maintain meaningful ownership is closing, and with Index and Kleiner now on the cap table the next round will be even more competitive.
tearSheet.action: Pre-commit reserve and signal intent to Index and Kleiner before the next round is visible to the market. The window narrows weekly. The 93/100 score justifies the highest reserve allocation in the portfolio.

alerts.message: firstminute did NOT participate in Series B (May 2025, $43M) or Series C (Mar 2026, $125M). Ownership has diluted from the seed. Reserve a position now before the next round prices competitively.

financials.notes: Seed to unicorn in under three years. The Series B to C step-up was 6x ($250M to $1.5B) in ten months, the fastest valuation trajectory in the portfolio.

Granola raises $125M to put your company's context to work

name: Tessl
growth: Spec Registry launched pre-populated with 10,000+ usage specs for popular libraries

tearSheet.thesis: A direct play on the agentic-coding wave, built by Guy Podjarny, founder of Snyk ($8.6B developer security company). Tessl translates natural-language specifications into secure, modular code. A $125M round for a company under a year old is the repeat-founder premium.
tearSheet.edge: Podjarny's second act. He has already built one of Europe's largest developer-tools companies. The spec-driven development paradigm positions Tessl above the model layer: the defensible asset is the specification, not the underlying LLM.
tearSheet.signal: PitchBook Opportunity Score 92/100, second-highest in the portfolio behind only Granola. Tessl was valued between $500M and $750M even before general release, and the Spec Registry already holds 10,000+ usage specs.
tearSheet.risk: A crowded, fast-moving AI coding category with well-funded competitors (Cursor, Replit, GitHub Copilot). Differentiation must be the specification layer. A 27% no-exit probability reflects the pre-PMF nature of the bet.
tearSheet.action: Confirm pro-rata terms ahead of the next round. Watch enterprise design-partner conversions as the leading indicator. A new fundraise is likely given the early-stage balance sheet.

alerts.message: Guy Podjarny is reported to have re-engaged with Snyk's board alongside his Tessl CEO role (2026); Tessl Registry uses Snyk for security scoring. Verify the board detail before relying on it.

founders.name: Guy Podjarny
founders.title: Founder and CEO
founders.background: Founder of Snyk ($8.6B developer security platform). Repeat founder with a proven track record in developer tooling.

firstminute.role: Participant in the $125M round alongside Index (lead), Accel, GV

financials.notes: Negative equity of about -$3.4M despite raising $125M, reflecting aggressive early burn. Zero debt. A follow-on raise is likely.

competitors.name: Snyk
competitors.notes: Podjarny's prior company, now an integration partner

signals.detail: Referring domains grew from near-zero to 936 in roughly five months. Presenting at CityJS London and AI Engineer Europe.

name: Storyblok
growth: 200,000+ developers and marketers in the community; $80M Series C closed Jun 2024

tearSheet.thesis: Brent Hoberman personally led this deal in December 2019. firstminute owns 13.32% of Storyblok, the largest ownership stake in any portfolio company, ahead of Wayve and n8n. PitchBook explicitly flags it as a likely acquisition target with 81% M&A probability.
tearSheet.edge: A tight cap table of eight investors. firstminute is the third-largest shareholder, behind Mubadala and Brighton Park Capital and ahead of both co-founders individually. Contentful was valued at $3B in 2021, so even a discounted exit returns a strong multiple on a roughly $2.5M initial investment.
tearSheet.signal: PitchBook acquisition-target flag, 88/100 Opportunity Score (matching Wayve), and 91% exit-success probability. Customers include Adidas, T-Mobile, and Renault across a 200,000-strong developer community.
tearSheet.risk: No valuation disclosed since the $80M Series C in June 2024. A mature headless-CMS category with credible competitors. Growth is share gain, not category creation.
tearSheet.action: Hold the position. Do not initiate fresh conviction, but do not underweight the 13.32% exposure, the most economically significant position in the portfolio on a per-share basis.

alerts.message: PitchBook flags Storyblok as a likely acquisition target (81% M&A probability). This is firstminute's largest single stake at 13.32%, a deal Brent led personally.

name: Taktile
primaryIndustry: Automated Risk Decisioning for Financial Services
growth: ARR up 3.5x year-on-year in 2024; customers across 24 markets

tearSheet.thesis: A platform for building automated risk and credit decisions, sold into banks and lenders. YC S20 alum. $79M+ raised, with a $54M Series B led by Balderton in Feb 2025. ARR grew 3.5x year-on-year and the product serves 24 markets.
tearSheet.edge: A best-in-class advisor roster: Daniel Dines (UiPath), Olivier Pomel (Datadog), Scott Chacon (GitHub), and Larry Summers (ex-US Treasury, OpenAI board, invested at Series B). Once embedded in a credit pipeline the product is sticky, with regulatory and operational switching costs.
tearSheet.signal: Customers now include Mercury, Zilch, Allianz, and Rakuten Bank. G2 Leader for multiple consecutive quarters, the product signal in a category governed by procurement rather than virality.
tearSheet.risk: Long enterprise procurement cycles in financial services. firstminute invested at seed but did not follow on at Series A or B. Growth depends on named reference accounts, not virality.
tearSheet.action: Hold. A tier-one lender reference would be the catalyst to revisit conviction upward. The one introduction that changes the story is a risk leader at a bulge-bracket bank.

name: Corvera
primaryIndustry: Agentic Supply Chain Operations for CPG Brands
customers: 12+ CPG brands signed within five weeks of first onboarding
growth: Signed more than a dozen CPG brands within five weeks of onboarding its first customer

tearSheet.thesis: A hands-free command centre automating supply chains and operations for fast-growing US and UK consumer-goods brands. YC W26. firstminute was the company's first investor, leading the $2M pre-seed in January 2026 before the $4.2M seed led by 6 Degrees Capital.
tearSheet.edge: CEO Chris Kong spent seven years in UK FMCG, scaling Better Nature to 5,000 stores before stepping back, and is a Forbes 30 Under 30 Europe honoree. CTO Dirk Breeuwer was Data and AI Lead at Google, leading AI transformation for Pixel.
tearSheet.signal: Referring domains climbed sharply alongside the May 2026 seed. The company signed more than a dozen CPG brands within five weeks of onboarding its first customer, an unusually fast early commercial signal.
tearSheet.risk: Earliest stage: eight employees, no historical financials, unproven at scale. Competitors Simple AI and Lance AI are at a similar stage. The next milestone is logo retention through a full operating cycle.
tearSheet.action: Track the first renewal cohort as the proof point ahead of any follow-on. Introduce to a scaled CPG operating partner; a reference buyer is the primary missing asset.

alerts.message: firstminute was Corvera's FIRST investor, leading the $2M pre-seed in Jan 2026. Lorcan Delaney (Principal) is the deal lead. High-ownership early position.

founders.name: Chris Kong
founders.title: Co-Founder and CEO
founders.background: 2x founder. Built Better Nature into a leading European tempeh brand, scaling to 5,000 stores before stepping back in 2025. Forbes 30 Under 30 Europe 2025. Relocated to San Francisco to build Corvera.

founders.name: Dirk Breeuwer
founders.title: Co-Founder and CTO
founders.background: Former Data and AI Lead at Google; led AI transformation for Google Pixel and built data infrastructure at Google scale.

financials.notes: Generating revenue at seed stage with no historical financials. Integrates with Shopify, Amazon Seller Central, NetSuite, and Snowflake. A voice-enabled agentic operating system for CPG supply chains.

name: Codewords
growth: Cody runs roughly 500,000 workflows per month; web traffic 4x in five months

tearSheet.thesis: Natural-language prompts build automations across CRM and business tools. firstminute co-led the original $4.23M seed in January 2024 and followed on in the $9M May 2026 seed extension. Current ownership is 10.26%, the second-largest institutional stake.
tearSheet.edge: Francois Chollet (Google AI, creator of Keras) is an angel in both rounds, alongside Amar Shah (Wayve co-founder). The core product, Cody, autonomously writes Python automations in plain English, distinct from visual no-code boxes, and already runs around 500K workflows a month.
tearSheet.signal: Opportunity Score 90/100, above Wayve and Mistral. Web traffic grew roughly 4x in five months. Angels include Miro's Andrey Khusid, ElevenLabs' Mati Staniszewski, and Supercell's Ilkka Paananen.
tearSheet.risk: Sits adjacent to n8n's category inside the same book. Worth a clean view on whether the two compound or compete for internal attention and resources. A 29% no-exit probability at a $45M post-money seed-stage valuation.
tearSheet.action: Map the overlap with n8n explicitly. Decide whether this is portfolio diversification or internal overlap before the next cheque. The 10.26% stake and 90/100 score justify holding.

alerts.message: 10.26% ownership makes firstminute the second-largest institutional shareholder. Sits in the same workflow-automation lane as n8n; internal overlap worth addressing explicitly.

name: Console
growth: Resolves over 50% of IT tickets autonomously; 4.7x valuation step-up in three months

tearSheet.thesis: AI agents that resolve IT tickets end-to-end are unbundling ServiceNow at the highest-volume, most measurable corner of a very large incumbent. Named directly in firstminute's published 2026 tech predictions, the strongest possible on-thesis signal.
tearSheet.edge: Ticket deflection has hard, board-visible ROI, and every resolved ticket is training data, so the moat compounds with scale. CEO Andrei Serban is a repeat founder who was previously an operator at Rippling. Customers already include Ramp, Scale AI, and Webflow.
tearSheet.signal: Opportunity Score 99/100, the single highest in the tracked dataset, placing Console in the top 1% globally. A 4.7x step-up from seed to Series A in three months, backed by Thrive and DST, with angels including Aaron Levie and Nikesh Arora.
tearSheet.risk: A crowded lane with credible challengers and ServiceNow shipping its own agents. The edge is execution and proprietary resolution data, not the idea. A 90% M&A probability suggests the acquirer is likely ServiceNow or a major IT platform.
tearSheet.action: Approach now, while still pre-consensus. Diligence the data flywheel and a named enterprise reference. Verify current round status: a 4.7x step-up in three months signals this is moving fast.

alerts.message: 99/100 Opportunity Score, the highest in the entire tracked dataset. firstminute named this exact category in its published 2026 predictions.

founders.background: Repeat founder; previously an operator at Rippling and earlier co-founded Fuzzbuzz. Brings enterprise relationships.

Angels include Box CEO Aaron Levie, Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora, and Quora CEO Adam D'Angelo.

signals.detail: Referring domains grew from 225 to 333 across Dec 2025-May 2026. Presented at the 2026 Gartner Digital Workplace Summit Americas. Customers include Ramp, Flock Safety, Scale AI, Webflow, Zip, and Calendly.

name: Omnea
growth: Revenue up 5x and headcount more than tripled in the past 12 months

tearSheet.thesis: Procurement is a large, manual back-office function that legacy suites digitised but never made intelligent, and AI-native is the rebuild. A $50M Series B in Sep 2025 led by Insight Partners and Khosla maps directly to firstminute's published call that compliance AI hits production at major institutions in 2026.
tearSheet.edge: The core team is a high-profile reunion of Tessian leadership: CEO, CFO, and CRO all from Tessian, which Proofpoint acquired. The GTM engine that built one successful B2B enterprise company has reassembled, and an industry-first MCP server brings live supplier data into Claude and ChatGPT.
tearSheet.signal: Opportunity Score 94/100. Revenue grew 5x with headcount more than tripling in twelve months. G2 Spring 2026 Leader in Procurement Orchestration. Customers include Spotify, Wise, MongoDB, and Monzo.
tearSheet.risk: Long enterprise sales cycles against entrenched incumbents (Coupa, SAP Ariba). It needs a sharp wedge workflow, not a full-suite assault on day one. An 87% M&A probability, with ZipHQ the category-defining US comparable.
tearSheet.action: First cheque. Underwrite the wedge workflow and early logo quality, not the eventual platform story. UK-founded, inside the fund's core geography, and the Tessian team has done this before.

name: Hadrian
hq: Torrance, CA (factories in CA, AZ, AL)
growth: 10x year-on-year growth since the Series B; revenue grew from $3M (2023) to $30M (2024)

tearSheet.thesis: Software-defined autonomous factories producing high-precision metal parts for rockets, submarines, and satellites. Revenue grew roughly 10x year-on-year, and the company reached a $1.6B valuation in late 2025. It plays directly into the defence and physical-AI reindustrialisation thesis.
tearSheet.edge: Opus, a proprietary software stack that automates quoting, programming, machining, and inspection. Three semi-autonomous factories are operational, with a new Mesa, AZ site and a submarine-parts facility in Alabama targeting the Columbia and Virginia-class programmes.
tearSheet.signal: A rare IPO-skewed exit profile at 56% IPO probability, reflecting the heavy asset base. The $260M Series C was led by Founders Fund and Lux Capital, with a16z and Altimeter participating, and a PitchBook estimate of $1.96B at very high confidence.
tearSheet.risk: Capital-intensive physical infrastructure: over $600M raised, including a large Series C. Defence supply chains carry high trust and liability bars. Primarily US-centric, which sits outside firstminute's lead geography, so a financial co-lead would be needed.
tearSheet.action: Initiate at measured size. Verify current round status and capitalisation. Confirm whether a European defence-manufacturing equivalent exists; the reindustrialisation thesis is compelling but the geography and capital scale require a co-lead.

founders.name: Christopher Power
founders.title: Founder and CEO
founders.background: Originally from Australia. Identified the bottleneck in the aging US precision-manufacturing base and founded Hadrian in late 2020 to rebuild it with software.

funding.leads: Founders Fund, Lux Capital (a16z, Altimeter); Morgan Stanley debt
Construct Capital, Founders Fund, Lux Capital

financials.notes: Revenue grew from $3M (2023) to $30M (2024), roughly 10x. Founders Fund and Lux led the $260M Series C in Jul 2025, with a $131M extension in Dec 2025 bringing the valuation to $1.6B. A rare IPO-skewed exit profile reflects the heavy asset base.

signals.detail: Referring domains stable at 500-540. Factory 3 in Mesa, AZ opened in early 2026; an Alabama submarine-parts facility targets the Columbia and Virginia-class nuclear submarine programmes.

name: Prem Labs
growth: Grew to roughly 105 employees by 2026; reportedly in talks for a $100M Series A

tearSheet.thesis: Secure, self-sovereign deployment of language models for organisations that cannot send data to a hyperscaler: banks, government, healthcare, and defence. The EU AI Act and data-residency rules create durable, non-discretionary demand. It sits directly on the speed, scale, and sovereignty thread from Brent's London Tech Week conversation with Peter Kyle.
tearSheet.edge: Confidential compute, cryptographic attestation, and zero vendor lock-in, running frontier models inside hardware-sealed Trusted Execution Environments. European-native is an advantage with the regulated buyer, not a constraint. David Maisel, founder of Marvel Studios, is an advisor and investor.
tearSheet.signal: Referring domains roughly doubled in five months, a strong backlink and developer-adoption signal. Bloomberg reports the company is in discussions to raise a $100M Series A, with Index Ventures and Sequoia Capital China already on the cap table.
tearSheet.risk: Capital-intensive infrastructure contested by Aleph Alpha and private hyperscaler offerings. A 41% no-exit probability reflects the crowded early enterprise-tooling state. Only about $19.5M raised so far.
tearSheet.action: First cheque, on thesis and on narrative. Pressure-test gross margin and the wedge customer. The $100M Series A in discussions is the timing signal: approach before it closes.

alerts.message: Reportedly in discussions to raise a $100M Series A. The approach window is open but closing. Index Ventures and Sequoia Capital China already on the cap table.

founders.name: Simone Giacomelli

founders.background: Serial entrepreneur. Co-founded SingularityNET with Ben Goertzel and founded Vulpem Ventures. Active in decentralised systems since 2013.

name: Avoca
primaryIndustry: Vertical AI Voice Agents for Home Services
growth: Surpassed eight figures in ARR in 2025; on track to book $1B in jobs

tearSheet.thesis: Voice AI agents automating inbound calls, scheduling, dispatch, and follow-up for HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and roofing trades. A $125M+ raise at a $1B valuation in April 2026, led by Meritech and General Catalyst, is a 4.4x step-up from Series A. Named directly in firstminute's 2026 tech predictions.
tearSheet.edge: The founding insight: a missed restaurant booking costs $50, but a single missed HVAC emergency call loses a $30,000-40,000 installation. The MIT founders co-designed in the field with a Dallas contractor before scaling, with Kleiner Perkins and General Catalyst on the cap table, plus ServiceTitan and Nexstar partnerships.
tearSheet.signal: Opportunity Score 94/100. A 4.4x valuation step-up in ten months and eight-figure ARR, on track to book $1B in jobs. Customers include Turnpoint, 1-800-GOT-JUNK, and Goettl.
tearSheet.risk: US-centric, outside the fund's lead geography of Europe and the UK. If pursued, this is likely a follow-on rather than a lead. Confirm whether a European or UK equivalent better fits the mandate.
tearSheet.action: Watch list. Do not initiate a lead position without a clear European mandate. Confirm whether a European home-services AI equivalent exists. If pursuing, treat it as a follow-on ticket, not a lead.

alerts.message: Named by firstminute in its published 2026 predictions, but US-centric. Confirm a European mandate or equivalent before approach; likely a follow-on, not a lead.
